prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under | Statement: Acceleration factors of 3-6 times in testing time were achieved at higher stress conditions compared to baseline of 65 C and 85% RH for adhesion stren
2. [GOLD] Source: Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under | Statement: Adhesion strength follows exponential decay model S = S0*exp(-t/t_char)^beta where degradation slope parameters depend on temperature and humidity str
3. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Elevated Lamination Temperatures on PV Module A | Statement: PV modules laminated at elevated temperatures up to 180 degrees C withstood DH3000 damp heat aging tests for peel force and yellowing.
4. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Elevated Lamination Temperatures on PV Module A | Statement: There exists a crosslinking saturation level above which gel content influence on adhesion becomes negligible.
5. [GOLD] Source: The Natural and Accelerated Evolution of EVA Adhesion Throug | Statement: At 10,000 hours of accelerated aging, EVA adhesion in mini-modules falls to a level generally seen after more than 15 years of field exposure.
